content.text: I'll read the file to show you the md-card pattern used in BeFirst Portal views.

Here is the complete content of `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Views\Position\_CreateOrUpdate.cshtml`, showing the **md-card pattern** used in BeFirst Portal views: ## Key md-card Pattern Structure ```html

Manage AspNetPosition: @Model.Position

``` ## Complete Pattern Breakdown **Basic Structure:** - **Outer container:** `
` - Material Design card wrapper - **Toolbar section:** `
` - Header/title area - **Heading:** `

` - Card title - **Content section:** `
` - Main body with form/content **Tabs Pattern (UIKit):** ```html
  • General
  • Roles
  • Users
``` **Form Elements Pattern:** ```html
@Html.LabelFor(y => y.Position) @Html.TextBoxFor(y => y.Position, new { @class = "md-input" })
``` **Button Toolbar Pattern (bottom of card):** ```html
Back
``` **JavaScript Inline Pattern:** - Always use `nonce="@Html.ScriptNonce()"` for CSP compliance - Scripts placed inline within tabs (not in `@section scripts`) **Grid Pattern:** - `@Html.AjaxGrid(Url.Action("GetRoles", "Position", new { id = Model.PositionID }))` - MvcGrid reload: `new MvcGrid(document.querySelector('#mvcRoles')).reload();` This pattern is consistent across BeFirst Portal views for create/edit forms with tabbed interfaces.
